slides.title: Python: Your New Superpower
slides.body: Before you can write Python programs, you need **Python installed** on your computer. Think of it like a music player — the code is the song, Python is the player that makes it run.
slides.tip: Python is free and works on Windows, macOS, and Linux.

slides.title: Download & Install in Minutes
slides.body: Grab the latest **Python 3** from [python.org/downloads](https://python.org/downloads). On **Windows**, tick **"Add Python to PATH"** on the first install screen — without it, your terminal won't find Python.
slides.code:
# After installing, open a terminal and type:
python --version
# or on macOS / Linux:
python3 --version
slides.tip: Any version starting with 3 means you're good to go.

slides.title: Pick Your Code Editor
slides.body: A **code editor** is your workshop for writing code. **VS Code** is free, beginner-friendly, and the most popular choice — just install it and add the Microsoft "Python" extension.
slides.tip: Think of Python as the engine and VS Code as the dashboard — the engine does the work, the dashboard makes it pleasant.

slides.title: Run Your First Python File
slides.body: Save your code in a file ending in **`.py`**, then run it from the terminal with `python3 filename.py`. That's all it takes.
slides.tip: Use `cd` in your terminal to navigate to the folder where your file is saved.

slides.title: pip Comes Free — You're Ready!
slides.body: Python bundles **pip**, a tool to install thousands of free libraries written by other developers. You won't need it today, but it's already waiting for you.
slides.tip: You now have a full Python environment — install, verify, edit, run. Let's write some code!
